News|UK

The Prince and Princess of Wales have joined theKing, Queen and Prime Minister in congratulating athletes as theParisOlympics draws to a close.

The Prince and Princess of Wales congratulatedTeam GBand said they are an “inspiration to us all” in a star-studded video message posted on social media.

It came ahead of the closing ceremonywhich began at the Stade de France at 9pm (8pm in the UK) on Sunday.

Great Britain has ended the Paris Olympics in seventh spot with 65 medals – the same amount won at London 2012.

US rapper Snoop Dogg started and finished the video shared by the Prince and Princess of Wales on Sunday night, which also included messages from other celebrities including David Beckham.

Kate said: “From all of us watching at home, congratulations to Team GB.”

William then added: “Well done on all you’ve achieved, you’ve been an inspiration to us all.”

Snoop Dogg started the video by saying “greetings loved ones” and ended it with the words “thank you Great Britain, on behalf of big Snoop Dogg, the prince and his lovely wife”.

KingCharlesalso shared a message on Sunday night which read: “My wife joins me in sending our warmest congratulations to the competitors and their support teams from Team GB and across the wholeCommonwealth, for their many outstanding successes at the Olympic Games in Paris.

“Your achievements, across so many disciplines, were forged from that invaluable combination of raw talent, true grit and hard toil over many years, burnished these past weeks by sportsmanship and team spirit in the finest tradition of the Games.

“To those who missed out on the greatest prizes, you have most certainly not fallen short in the pride that you, too, have generated for your nations.

“As we thankFrancefor hosting such a wonderful tournament and look to the Paralympics ahead, I can only say that you have all been an inspiration.”

The Prime Minister has also congratulated Team GB’s athletes.

Sir Keir Starmer posted on X, formerly Twitter: “To all our incredible @TeamGB athletes and support staff, well done and congratulations.

Hottest hotel openings for 2026

Le Manoir appoints new three-star chef as two-star Raymond Blanc steps back

Jobs crisis for London as unemployment rate doubles in less than two years

Reset your wellbeing for the year ahead

“You’re bringing home more than just medals. You’ve inspired a new generation of athletes while entertaining a nation. Thank you.”

The Paris Olympics closing ceremony kicked off with a range of performances, and thousands of Olympians poured into the stadium with their home countries, before joining together for some karaoke.

The sing-along section included Les Champs-Elysees, Gala Rizzatto’s Freed From Desire and Queen’s classic We Are The Champions.

French indie rock band Phoenix, electro house artist Kavinsky and Vampire Weekend frontman Ezra Koenig were then among the performers during the lively music section of the ceremony.

Vampire Weekend star Koenig then joined Phoenix to begin the handover to the States for the 2028 Los Angeles Games.

Later on, the show was expected to see performances from Californian natives Billie Eilish, the Red Hot Chili Peppers and Snoop Dogg as Paris passes the Olympic torch to 2028 hosts Los Angeles.

Great Britainended the Paris Olympics with 65 medals– the same amount won at London 2012 and second-best ever tally on foreign soil – with only the United States (126) and China (91) claiming more.

But Team GB won eight fewer gold medals than in Tokyo three years ago and were 13 down on the 27 collected at Rio 2016.

A final return of 14 gold, 22 silver and 29 bronzesaw Britain slip to seventh spot in the medal table – its lowest place since finishing 10th at Athens 2004 – and third-best European nation behind hosts France and the Netherlands.

Traditional Olympic sports such as athletics, cycling and swimming returned medals, while rowing banished its Tokyo nightmare to win three golds in a strong regatta.

But newer sports such as skateboarding, taekwondo and trampolining also had success, while Team GB won its first-ever medals in sport climbing.

Team GB have already made a start to planning for the Los Angeles 2028 Games by securing Stanford University as its exclusive preparation camp.

title: Paris Olympics: Kate and William share video congratulating Team GB as Games close | The Standard
author: Jordan Reynolds, Lydia Chantler-Hicks
description: They joined a host of celebrities including David Beckham in congratulating the athletes
keywords: King, Charles, Commonwealth, Paris, Team GB, France
